```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

`= ⟪x, (f(a) x_1) y⟫_ℂ + ⟪x, (f(a) y) y⟫_ℂ`
`= ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/

    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.

    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

The main goal is to prove the linearity of the integral with respect to the vector `x`. Specifically, we need to show that for any `x_1, y : H`:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (x_1 + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

This is a direct consequence of the linearity of the inner product. The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x, y}` is defined as `⟪x, (f(a)) y⟫_ℂ`. Therefore, we have:
`⟪x, (f(a) (x_1 + y)) y⟫_ℂ = ⟪x, (f(a) x_1) y + f(a) y) y⟫_ℂ`
`= ⟪x, (f(a) x_1) y⟫_ℂ + ⟪x, (f(a) y) y⟫_ℂ`
`= ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

  sorry

Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by

The goal is to prove the linearity of the integral with respect to the second argument `y`.
Specifically, we need to show that for any `x_1, y : H`, the following holds:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (x_1 + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x y) f`

This is true because the integral is linear in the measure. The proof in Lean will involve applying the appropriate theorems about complex measures and their integrals.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry

lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry

-/

noncomputable section

  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/

  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This

    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'

            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.

The main goal is to prove the linearity of the integral with respect to the argument `x₁`:
`∀ (x₁ y : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (x₁ + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x₁) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

To prove this, we need to show that the integral of `f` with respect to `E_{a, x, x₁+y}` is the sum of the integrals with respect to `E_{a, x, x₁}` and `E_{a, x, y}`.

From the definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure`, we know that `E_{a, x, y}` is the complex measure associated with the linear functional `L(b) = ⟨x, b·y⟩`. The linearity of this functional in `y` is clear: `L(y₁+y₂) = ⟨x, (y₁+y₂)·a⟩ = ⟨x, y₁·a⟩ + ⟨x, y₂·a⟩`. 

Since the integral of a linear functional with respect to a measure is linear in the argument of the functional, we have:
`∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x₁+y} (z) = ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x₁} (z) + ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, y} (z)`

In Lean, this should follow from properties of `ComplexMeasure.integral` and the linearity of the underlying functional. Let's try to prove it directly:

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

The goal is to prove that for any `x_1, y : H`, the following holds:

homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This

cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

Now, we need to complete the `map_add'` part of the `toBoundedBilin` definition.

The goal is to prove that for any `x_1, y : H`, the following holds:
`ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x (x_1 + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H ... x y) f`

This is a standard property of measures. Specifically, for a fixed `y`, the map `x ↦ E_{a, x, y}` should be linear.

To prove this, we need to show that the map `x ↦ ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f` is linear.

Let's write the proof:

We need to prove the `map_add'` property for `toBoundedBilin`. Let's expand the definition:

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

We will now provide the proof for the `map_add'` property. This property requires showing that for any `x_1, y : H`, the integral of `f` against `E_{a, x, x_1+y}` equals the sum of the integrals of `f` against `E_{a, x, x_1}` and `E_{a, x, y}`.

The key insight is that `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is constructed via `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` from a linear functional. The linearity of the integral with respect to the function `f` is a standard property of the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani integral.

    ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H (inst : NormedAddCommGroup H) (inst : InnerProductSpace ℂ H) (inst : CompleteSpace H) a ha x (x_1 + y)) f =

        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x

        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)

      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring

import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity

          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

by

The goal is to prove that the integral is linear in the vectors `x` and `y`. Specifically, we need to show:
`∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x_1 + y}(z) = ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x_1}(z) + ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, y}(z)`

This is a property of complex measures. When the measure is defined from a linear functional, the integral should be linear in the vectors.

def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

The goal is to prove that the integral is linear in `x` and `y`. Specifically, we need to show:
`∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x_1 + y}(z) = ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, x_1}(z) + ∫ f(z) dE_{a, x, y}(z)`

This is a property of complex measures. When the measure is defined from a linear functional, the integral should be linear in the vectors.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom,

(hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

The goal is to complete the `sorry` in the proof of `map_add'` for `toBoundedBilin`.
The statement to be proved is:
`∀ (x_1 y : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (x_1 + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

This is a direct application of the linearity of the integral with respect to the measure.
Let's complete the proof.

The goal is to complete the `sorry` in the proof of `map_add'` for `toBoundedBilin`.
The statement to be proved is:
`∀ (x_1 y : H), ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x (x_1 + y)) f = ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x x_1) f + ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) f`

This is a direct application of the linearity of the integral with respect to the measure.
Let's complete the proof.

import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/

    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'

            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,

              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

@[simp]

    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/

    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/

  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)

The goal is to prove the linearity of the integral with respect to the second argument `y`.
This is a direct application of the linearity of the integral with respect to the measure.
If `μ` is a complex measure and `f` is a bounded Borel function, then `∫ f d(μ_1 + μ_2) = ∫ f dμ_1 + ∫ f dμ_2`.
In our case, `E_{a, x, y}` is linear in `y`, so its integral is linear in `y`.

-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound `toLinearFunctional_bounded` and the fact that
`f => f(a)` is an isometry, `norm_cfcHom`. -/
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound (x y : H) : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
  · positivity
  · intro f
    simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y⟫_ℂ‖
        ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y‖ := norm_inner_le_norm x
          (((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ * ‖y‖) :=
        mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (le_opNorm ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f) y)
          (norm_nonneg x)
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f)‖ := by ring
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f]

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, one obtains a complex measure `E_{a, x ,y}` from the continuous linear
functional `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` by the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem.
cf. Rudin "Functiona Analysis" the proof of Theorem 12.22, above formula (5).
-/
def cfc_toComplexMeasure (x y : H) := ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
  C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
  (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
  (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)

/-- The integral of `f` against `E_{a, x,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`. -/
lemma cfc_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  sorry

/-- The complex measure `E_{a, x,y}` has the total variation bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
Rudin "Real and complex analysis" Theorem 6.19 (2), combined with the bound by
`cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound`.-/
lemma cfc_variation_toComplexMeasure_bounded (x y : H) :
    (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).variation Set.univ ≤ ‖x‖ₑ * ‖y‖ₑ := by
  sorry

/-- The integral of a Borel function `f` against `E_{a, x ,y}` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
We should have an analogue of `MeasureTheory.lintegral_le_const` for `ComplexMeasure.integral`.
-/
lemma bfc_bounded (x y : H) {C : ℝ} {f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ}  (hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C)
    (hX : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f) :
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * C := by
  sorry

/-- The linear functional from bounded Borel functions `f` to `∫ f z ∂E_{a, x, y} z` as a bounded
linear functional (bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`). All the remaining properties should follow from
the definition and `bfc_bounded`. -/
def toBoundedBilin {C : ℝ} (f : (spectrum ℂ a) → ℂ)
  --(hf_bounded : ∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ C) (hf_measurable : @Measurable _ _ (borel (spectrum ℂ a)) _ f)
  : H →L⋆[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] ℂ where
  toFun x := {  toFun y := (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f
                map_add' := sorry
```

The goal is to prove the linearity of the integral with respect to the second argument `y`.
This is a direct application of the linearity of the integral with respect to the measure.
If `μ` is a complex measure and `f` is a bounded Borel function, then `∫ f d(μ_1 + μ_2) = ∫ f dμ_1 + ∫ f dμ_2`.
In our case, `E_{a, x, y}` is linear in `y`, so its integral is linear in `y`.

```tactics
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM
